channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Tyler Pager, Farnaz Fassihi and Erika Solomon

item.title: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
item.description: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Facing Hard-Liners at Home and Bluster From Trump, Iran Sends Mixed Signals on Peace Talks
item.description: Publicly, Iranian officials refuse to confirm a second round of negotiations with the United States, even as they privately say they are making plans to attend.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman walking by a mural showing Iranian missiles attacking a U.S. ship, in Tehran in April.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Talks Appear on Track to Resume Despite Mixed Messages
item.description: With the two-week cease-fire almost over, Vice President JD Vance was expected to head to Pakistan on Tuesday for the second round of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Farnaz Fassihi, Tyler Pager and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance in Islamabad, Pakistan, this month during a previous round of negotiations between the United States and Iran.

item.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
item.description: The container ship, the Touska, was sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury over links to Iranian weapons programs.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A screenshot of a video posted by U.S. Central Command on Sunday that was described as showing a U.S. Navy destroyer near an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Arabian Sea.

item.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
item.description: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, last week.

item.title: In Lebanon, residents of the south make their way home over signs of war.
item.description: Some of the million people displaced by the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon are flooding back home since a 10-day truce between Israel and Lebanon took hold.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder
item.media:description: A Lebanese boy is welcomed by his aunt and uncle after crossing a destroyed bridge with other family members, in the background. They were crossing over the Litani River in the south of Lebanon near the village of Tayr Felsay, on Monday.

item.title: Democratic Senators Fault Hegseth’s Leadership on Iran
item.description: In a letter, the 11 senators questioned the defense secretary’s decision to gut programs intended to protect civilians and said his orders endangered U.S. troops.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: The senators’ letter said several of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s actions violated federal and international laws.

item.title: Israeli Soldier in Lebanon Sledgehammered a Statue of Jesus
item.description: The military is investigating the soldier. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed regret for any hurt caused to “believers in Lebanon and around the world.”
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Is at a Standstill Again
item.description: Few vessels are crossing after Iran reversed course on reopening the vital waterway and ships came under attack.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships and boats in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Monday.

item.title: A Leaner Saudi Arabia Turns From Grandiose Plans to Pragmatism
item.description: A decade after Mohammed bin Salman unveiled his “Vision 2030” program to transform the country’s economy, the kingdom is facing financial strains and reassessing its trajectory.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Saudi crown prince, Mohammed bin Salman, in Washington last fall. The prince’s plans and ambitions have grown more rapidly than the state’s financial capacity.

item.title: Vance Heads to New Talks With Iran. At Stake: Peace and His Own Standing.
item.description: The vice president is again center stage, after abruptly leaving the first round of high-level Iranian peace talks without an agreement.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance last week in Georgia. He is returning to Pakistan this week, after spending 21 hours last weekend negotiating with the Iranians.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Farnaz Fassihi and Max Bearak

item.title: In Turkey, Middle Powers Ponder Diplomacy With a Rogue U.S.
item.description: The U.S. remains an essential player. The problem, one analyst said, is how to deal effectively with a power that is “indispensable, coercive and unpredictable at the same time.”
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey in Antalya on Friday, where he said the global system was in “a moral and existential crisis.”

item.title: U.S. Fires on Iranian Cargo Ship Touska in Arabian Sea
item.description: The military said it had disabled the vessel after it ignored repeated warnings to stop, amid a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports. Marines boarded the ship and seized it.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A screenshot of a video posted by U.S. Central Command that was described as showing a U.S. Navy destroyer warning and then firing on an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Arabian Sea on Sunday.

item.title: Can U.S. Blockade Iranian-Linked Ships Anywhere in the World? Yes, But …
item.description: Maritime and military law experts say an expansion of the naval blockade announced last week raises legal and practical questions but has ample historical precedent.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tankers anchored off Iran on Saturday.

item.title: Energy Secretary Says Gas Prices May Stay Above $3 Until 2027
item.description: Secretary of Energy Chris Wright’s acknowledgment in a TV interview undercut President Trump’s earlier claim that price increases would be “short-term.”
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim and Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The price of gas has increased significantly since the war with Iran began in late February.

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Cargo Ship as Both Countries Claim Cease-Fire Violations
item.description: President Trump said the United States attacked an Iranian ship that tried to get past the U.S. blockade, hours after Iran said the blockade was a war crime that violated the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporter on Air Force One.

item.title: Hamas Officials Say Group Is Ready to Hand Over Some Weapons
item.description: Two senior Hamas officials in Gaza said the group was prepared to relinquish some automatic rifles and other arms, a concession that falls short of Israeli-U.S. demands.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer and Adam Rasgon
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Armed members of Hamas in Nuseirat, Gaza, last year.

item.title: Iran Eases Some Internet Restrictions, as Wider Blackout Passes 50th Day
item.description: Critics say Iran may be creating a “tiered internet” model, where access is limited to the politically and economically privileged.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Two women with their phones in Tehran, Iran, on Thursday.

item.title: Book Review: ‘When We See You Again,’ by Rachel Goldberg-Polin
item.description: Rachel Goldberg-Polin’s precise and devastating memoir chronicles the 328 days her son was held hostage in Gaza, and what came after.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Jacobs
item.media:description: Rachel Goldberg-Polin during her remarks at the 2024 Democratic National Convention, where she called for help in getting her son released after 320 days.

item.title: In Qatar, Trapped Between the U.S. and Iran, War Forced a Reckoning
item.description: The gas-rich Gulf nation is in a state of “strategic shock” after the war dealt a serious blow to its economy, sending ripples around the world.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Qatar, though on good terms with both the United States and Iran, has been harmed economically by the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran and endured retaliatory attacks from Tehran.

item.title: Syrian Billionaires Needed a Favor in Washington. They Invoked the Trump Name.
item.description: The attempt by the Khayyats to influence foreign policy while discussions are underway about potential Trump family deals is an increasingly common feature of the president’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Eric Lipton
item.media:credit: Laura Boushnak for The New York Times
item.media:description: Moutaz and Ramez Al-Khayyat, two of three Syrian-born brothers, at their company’s headquarters in Doha, Qatar.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Shirin Hakim and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: Hezbollah Is Willing to Cooperate With a Cease-Fire With Israel. For Now.
item.description: The leader of the Iran-backed militia said that a more durable cease-fire with Israel would require the fulfillment of a list of longstanding demands.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Protesters waved an Iranian flag and flags for Hezbollah in downtown Beirut during a demonstration to show support for Hezbollah last week.

item.title: Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran Declares Strict Control of Vital Shipping Route
item.description: The day after Iran declared the vital waterway open, it reversed course, injecting new peril into navigation there.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

item.title: More Displaced Lebanese Head Home as Cease-Fire Is Tested
item.description: The truce appeared to mostly hold even as Israel said it had carried out strikes on what it called “terrorists” approaching its forces in southern Lebanon. Separately, a U.N. peacekeeper was killed.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Sarah Chaayto and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Lebanese residents returned to their homes in the south on Saturday.

item.title: Reopening Strait of Hormuz Would Ease Oil Crisis but Only So Much
item.description: Analysts said energy and shipping companies would be reluctant to fully restore operations until they were confident that hostilities were over.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An oil tanker off Basra, Iraq, on Friday. If the Strait of Hormuz were to fully open, it would take weeks for substantial amounts of Persian Gulf oil and gas to reach buyers around the world.

item.title: For Iran, Flexing Control Over Waterway Is New Deterrent
item.description: Iran’s government could emerge from the conflict with a blueprint to keep adversaries at bay, regardless of any restrictions on its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Adam Entous and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A satellite image showing ships’ movements in a section of the Strait of Hormuz this month.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Euan Ward, Leily Nikounazar and Somini Sengupta

item.title: A Potent Threat in Strait of Hormuz: Iran’s ‘Mosquito Fleet’
item.description: Although much of the regular Iranian navy is destroyed, the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps can still deploy small, speedy boats to disrupt shipping.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps speedboat and navy ship in the Persian Gulf in 2024.

item.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
item.description: The Trump administration has loosened restrictions on Russian oil exports since the war in the Middle East began to rattle energy markets in March.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Wednesday that the U.S. government would not renew the sanctions exemption on Russian oil already at sea.

item.title: Trump Frames Iran War as All but Over in Optimistic Social Media Flurry
item.description: Iranian officials did not confirm most of Mr. Trump’s claims and disputed several of them.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump during an event in Las Vegas on Thursday.

item.title: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open, but adds that it is still under Tehran’s supervision.
item.description: Ab
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Attendees at a rally in support of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Amid Conflicting Messages From Trump and Iran, Hopes for Peace Deal Emerge
item.description: On a day when both Iran and the United States declared the Strait of Hormuz opened, hopes for an agreement rose. But statements from President Trump and Iranian leaders about negotiations were sometimes at odds.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Peter Eavis, Pranav Baskar and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in support of Iran’s leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: The U.S. blockade on ships will continue until Trump ends it, says Central Command chief.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Adm. Brad Cooper, the head of U.S. Central Command, during a news conference at the Pentagon on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Is Urged to Move on Nuclear Site Thought to Be Beyond Reach of Bombs
item.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: A satellite image provided by Vantor shows the tunnel complex at Pickaxe Mountain in Iran last month. Last fall, satellite images revealed that Iran had advanced construction at the site soon after U.S. forces disabled the country’s three main nuclear facilities in June.

item.title: Europe Wants to Help Restore Shipping in the Strait. It Just Isn’t Sure How.
item.description: Amid conflicting reports about the situation in the Strait of Hormuz, European leaders gathered on Friday to coordinate a plan to guard it.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left, Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, President Emmanuel Macron of France, Prime Minister Keir Starmer of the United Kingdom and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy arriving for a conference on navigation through the Strait of Hormuz at the Élysée Palace in Paris on Friday.

item.title: Why Lebanon’s Government Has Struggled to Contain Hezbollah
item.description: Lebanon’s government has been caught between Western demands to disarm the Shiite Muslim militant group and fears of inflaming sectarian tensions.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Hezbollah flag at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, last month.

item.title: Oil Prices Fall Sharply After Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is Open
item.description: But analysts said it was not clear how quickly the oil industry in the Persian Gulf would be able to get back to normal.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.K. Police Investigate Claims Drones Targeted Israeli Embassy in London
item.description: The Metropolitan Police said that the building had not been attacked but that they were assessing “discarded items” in Kensington Gardens, a nearby park, which was cordoned off.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Officers in Kensington Gardens in London on Friday.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Is Open During Cease-Fire in Lebanon, U.S. and Iran Say
item.description: Statements from President Trump and Iran aimed to raise confidence in the safety of the waterway, but shipping experts said risks remained.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Jenny Gross and Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo ships line up in the Strait of Hormuz as seen from the United Arab Emirates in March.

item.title: ‘I Just Want to Be Back’: Thousands Rush South in Lebanon Under Cease-Fire
item.description: Lebanese people who had been displaced by fighting expressed a mix of excitement and uncertainty about a pause in Israel’s campaign against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Hwaida Saad and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire: What to Know
item.description: The pause in fighting would remove a major hindrance to the U.S.-Iran peace talks, if it holds. New talks are set for Thursday in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Families make their way home on Friday after the announcement of a cease-fire in Lebanon.

item.title: In northern Israel, relief at the cease-fire is overshadowed by unease.
item.description: Residents are wary that the truce may offer only a temporary reprieve after weeks of rocket fire by Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A street in the northern city of Kiryat Shmona, near Israel’s border with Lebanon, on Friday.
